creator: @quebert
executive_summary: May 13 2026 — Tododeia moderate run. Market regime MIXED: VIX=18.06, Fear & Greed=42 (Fear), synthetic=71.1. 35/59 tickers pass RSI+entry screen — broadest oversold cluster in 2 weeks driven by tech and consumer de-rating. NOW reclaims #1 slot on price-target raise + excellent oversold entry; META and PLTR maintain thesis with AI monetization intact. Financials: BAC and JPM held per user preference (C excluded). MSFT remains invalidated — OpenAI trial continues (Altman on stand today). NU binary event TONIGHT: Q1 2026 earnings 6PM EDT — position hard-capped 4%. Portfolio: 10 picks, 20% cash buffer maintained, correlation enforced.

macro_environment.summary: MIXED regime: VIX 18.06 signals moderate uncertainty. Fear & Greed at 42 indicates elevated caution but not panic — historically positive for medium-term mean reversion in oversold quality names. CPI 3.8% regime still in effect; higher-for-longer supports bank NIM expansion (BAC, JPM thesis). OpenAI vs Musk litigation ongoing — MSFT litigation overhang active (Altman testifying). Oil elevated — consumer discretionary (HD) remains binary-event risk ahead of May 19 earnings.
macro_environment.interest_rate_outlook: Higher-for-longer confirmed — Fed cut window closed post-CPI 3.8%. NIM expansion favors large-cap banks through H2 2026.
macro_environment.inflation_outlook: CPI 3.8% YoY (3-year high). Core 0.4% MoM sticky. Stagflationary overlay persists — defensive healthcare (AMGN) and quality growth (PLTR, NOW) preferred over rate-sensitive growth.
macro_environment.geopolitical_risk: Oil elevated — Iran ceasefire fragile. Stagflationary cost pressure on consumer discretionary. HD earnings May 19 doubly risky. No new escalation today.
key_factors: VIX 18.06 — moderate fear, not panic; mean-reversion window opening for oversold quality names
CPI 3.8% higher-for-longer — NIM tailwind for BAC/JPM, headwind for high-multiple growth
MSFT litigation active — OpenAI trial, Altman on stand; avoidance maintained until Q3 2026
NU Q1 2026 earnings TONIGHT (May 14 6PM EDT) — binary event, position capped at 4%
NOW price target raised — ServiceNow AI growth story gaining analyst conviction
BAC AI bond trading role expansion (LTX partnership) — incremental bullish catalyst

cross_sector_insights.insight: NOW price target raised + excellent-oversold RSI=33.5 with PEG=0.88 creates highest risk/reward in the portfolio today
cross_sector_insights.implication: NOW reclaims #1 rank — AI workflow automation monetization underappreciated at current entry; thesis confirmed by analyst upgrades

cross_sector_insights.insight: MSFT litigation overhang continues (OpenAI trial, Altman testifying) — not yet a re-entry candidate
cross_sector_insights.implication: Maintain full invalidation. PLTR and NOW continue to absorb AI software rotation capital displaced from MSFT

cross_sector_insights.insight: BAC deepens AI bond trading role (LTX partnership) — incremental structural advantage in fixed income automation
cross_sector_insights.implication: BAC thesis strengthens beyond NIM tailwind — AI-enabled trading desk is a long-cycle revenue compounder; maintain overweight

cross_sector_insights.insight: NU RSI=27.2 excellent-oversold ahead of Q1 earnings TONIGHT — market pricing in elevated risk pre-print
cross_sector_insights.implication: Oversold entry pre-earnings is a double-edged setup; position hard-capped at 4%. Strong buy consensus ($19.87 target vs $13.27) but binary risk is real

cross_sector_insights.insight: MELI Q1 showed margin compression despite revenue growth — strategic investment cycle impacting near-term earnings
cross_sector_insights.implication: Thesis intact (LatAm e-commerce + fintech) but margin recovery timeline extends to Q3 2026; size at 7% not 10%

risk_adjusted_picks.name: ServiceNow
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Excellent oversold entry (RSI=33.5), PEG=0.88 — growth at discount. Price target raised today by analyst. AI agentic workflow monetization accelerating. Bullish news flow. Strong buy consensus at $145 target vs $89 entry (+63% upside). Downtrend in RSI means dip is technical, not fundamental.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: ServiceNow is the operating system for enterprise AI workflows. Agentic AI expansion (Now Assist) is driving net new ARR across ITSM, HRSD and CSM. Price target raised reflects accelerating AI attach rate. RSI=33.5 oversold at PEG<1 is a high-quality dip.
thesis_invalidators: Revenue miss or guidance cut in Q2 2026
Enterprise AI budget freeze
Salesforce or Microsoft aggressive pricing on workflow automation

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Meta Platforms
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Excellent oversold entry (RSI=25.4), PEG=0.89 — deeply cheap for AI-monetization leader. Strong buy consensus target $826.69 vs $603 entry (+37%). Insider distribution is normal management selling, not distress. AI-native ad stack is a structural earnings compounder.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Meta's AI-powered ad targeting and Llama open-source strategy create durable moats. Reality Labs losses declining as AR/VR monetization improves. RSI=25.4 represents a multi-month oversold extreme — historically strong mean-reversion setup at PEG<1.
thesis_invalidators: FTC antitrust action on Instagram/WhatsApp
AI compute cost spiral exceeding revenue growth
Regulatory ad targeting restrictions in EU/US

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Palantir Technologies
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Excellent oversold (RSI=31.0), PEG=2.05 justified by 30%+ growth trajectory. Buy consensus $183 target vs $136 (+34.6%). Bullish news — multiple 'AI stock crushing Nvidia' narratives emerging. Insider mixed but not alarming. MSFT litigation validates Palantir's non-OpenAI AI stack differentiation.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Palantir's AIP (AI Platform) commercial expansion is accelerating — US commercial revenue growing 55% YoY. Government contracts provide revenue floor while commercial side compounds. MSFT litigation creates AI stack diversification demand that benefits Palantir directly.
thesis_invalidators: Federal contract non-renewal or budget sequester
Commercial AIP churn above 15%
Multiple compression if rate environment worsens sharply

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Bank of America
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Good entry RSI=36.1, PEG=0.92 — exceptional value for a Tier-1 bank. Strong buy consensus target $62.97 vs $50.78 (+24%). Bullish catalyst: AI bond trading partnership (LTX) expands fixed income automation edge. CPI 3.8% higher-for-longer is a direct NIM tailwind. User preference confirmed: BAC over C.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: BAC benefits from a multi-quarter NIM expansion cycle as higher-for-longer rates keep spread income elevated. New AI-powered bond trading (LTX partnership) is a structural revenue add. Trading revenue record highs in Q1 2026 demonstrated earnings power. PEG=0.92 is deeply undervalued for a top-3 US bank.
thesis_invalidators: Fed emergency cut cycle (inverts NIM thesis)
Credit deterioration in commercial real estate book
Regulatory capital requirement increase

risk_adjusted_picks.name: MercadoLibre
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Oversold RSI=28.8, PEG=1.01. Strong buy consensus $2305 target vs $1578 (+46%). Q1 margin compression noted (strategic investment cycle) but revenue growth intact. Baillie Gifford block-sale pressure subsiding. LatAm e-commerce + Mercado Pago fintech are multi-decade compounders.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: MercadoLibre is the Amazon + PayPal of Latin America operating in a structurally underpenetrated market. Mercado Pago is the leading digital wallet in Brazil/Mexico with 50M+ MAUs. Strategic investment cycle (logistics, fintech expansion) compresses near-term margins but builds a 5-year revenue moat.
thesis_invalidators: Brazilian currency (BRL) devaluation >20%
Regulatory fintech restrictions in Brazil or Argentina
Q2 revenue miss signaling competitive share loss

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Netflix
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Excellent oversold RSI=32.0, PEG=1.28. Buy consensus $114.56 target vs $87.66 (+30.7%). $325B economic impact narrative reinforces platform moat. Insider distribution is normal. Lawsuit keyword in news is existing content licensing — not material to thesis.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Netflix's ad-supported tier is now the primary growth driver — subscriber monetization expanding beyond pure subscription. $325B content spend economic impact announcement reinforces pricing power narrative. Password sharing crackdown continues to convert free users to paid. RSI=32 dip is an accumulation opportunity.
thesis_invalidators: Subscriber loss in Q2 exceeding 1M
Ad-tier ARPU declining below projections
Major content overspend writedown

risk_adjusted_picks.name: JPMorgan Chase
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Good entry RSI=41.4, PEG=1.60. Buy consensus $342 target vs $304.88 (+12.2%). Tokenized fund launch (JPMorgan blockchain) is a next-cycle revenue optionality. Insider distribution (11 sells) reflects normal management diversification — Dimon historically sells on schedule. User preference confirmed: JPM over GS/MS.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: JPMorgan is the best-in-class US bank with diversified revenue across investment banking, trading, consumer and CIB. Tokenized fund filing is an early-mover advantage in digital asset institutional products. Higher-for-longer rates support NIM for another 2-3 quarters. London tower relocation commentary is noise, not strategy.
thesis_invalidators: CRE loan loss acceleration
IB revenue collapse from deal freeze
Regulatory capital surcharge increase

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Amgen
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Good entry RSI=42.5, PEG=2.19 — rich but justified for a dividend-paying defensive biotech. May 12 #1 pick +1.52% confirms stagflation rotation thesis. CPI 3.8% regime benefits defensive healthcare. Dividend yield attracts capital rotation from rate-sensitive growth names.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Amgen is a defensive biotech compounder with best-in-class dividend yield (3.2%) and a diversified pipeline (obesity, oncology, rare disease). In a CPI 3.8% higher-for-longer environment, dividend-paying defensives attract institutional rotation. RSI=42.5 near-support entry with stagflation macro tailwind intact.
thesis_invalidators: Pipeline failure in obesity program (AMG 133)
Pricing pressure legislation (IRA drug price negotiation expansion)
FDA clinical hold on major asset

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Home Depot
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Oversold RSI=27.2, buy consensus $406 target vs $310 (+31%). Earnings ~May 19 (6 days) is a binary risk. Oil elevated adds cost pressure. News headline 'share price weakness — is it time to revisit?' is consistent with accumulation zone. Position sized down to 6% given earnings proximity.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Home Depot is the dominant US home improvement retailer with a structurally resilient contractor channel. RSI=27.2 extreme oversold at historically strong support. Dividend aristocrat with 25+ year growth record. Post-earnings clarity (May 19) is the catalyst to full-size. Pro contractor business remains robust despite consumer spending moderation.
thesis_invalidators: Q1 earnings miss with negative same-store sales guidance
Housing market further deterioration
Oil above $110 causing consumer spending retraction

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Nu Holdings
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Excellent oversold RSI=27.2, PEG=0.81, strong buy $19.87 target vs $13.27 (+49.7%). BUT: Q1 2026 earnings TONIGHT (May 14 6PM EDT). News keywords 'strong/miss/growth' signal asymmetric uncertainty. Berkshire connection (Abel may buy) provides institutional credibility. HARD CAP at 4% — do not add before earnings print.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Nu Holdings is the largest digital bank in Latin America with 100M+ customers and still-underpenetrated addressable market in Brazil, Mexico and Colombia. PEG=0.81 is deeply cheap for a 50%+ revenue growth fintech. RSI=27.2 pre-earnings is attractive but the binary event risk requires position discipline. Altman Z=1.8 (distress zone) is a structural watch item.
thesis_invalidators: Q1 earnings miss on NII or credit quality deterioration
Brazilian regulatory action on digital banking
NPL acceleration above 7%

historical_accuracy.notable: 80% positive return rate May 12→13. AMGN +1.52%, JPM +2.36%, MELI +1.92% led. NOW -0.79% and NU -1.78% were the two misses — NOW thesis confirmed by price target raise today; NU miss attributed to pre-earnings fear ahead of tonight's Q1 print. MSFT invalidation from May 12 (OpenAI litigation) confirmed correct — trial ongoing with Altman on stand today. May 12 defensive rotation (AMGN, BAC, JPM) proved prescient in MIXED macro regime.

warnings: MSFT INVALIDATED (ACTIVE): OpenAI litigation continues — Sam Altman on stand today. No re-entry before Q3 2026. RSI=35.1 is a trap entry while litigation risk is unresolved.
NU BINARY EVENT TONIGHT: Q1 2026 earnings May 14 6PM EDT — position HARD-CAPPED at 4%. Do NOT add before print. News keywords 'miss/strong' signal genuine uncertainty.
HD EARNINGS RISK: Earnings ~May 19 (6 days). Position sized down to 6%. Oil elevated adds cost pressure. Consider reducing further on any pre-earnings gap-up.
MELI MARGIN COMPRESSION: Q1 deep dive confirms strategic investment cycle compressing margins. Revenue growth intact but earnings recovery timeline H2 2026. Monitor Q2 margins closely.
CPI MACRO REGIME: 3.8% YoY higher-for-longer confirmed. High-multiple growth (PLTR 65x FwdPE) remains vulnerable to rate shock. Keep PLTR at 9% not higher.
CORRELATION ENFORCED: Big tech capped at META+PLTR (MSFT invalidated). Financials capped at BAC+JPM (C excluded per user preference). Healthcare: AMGN only (REGN/JNJ cut).
SOFI HARD-EXCLUDED: Hold rating + Truist price target cut to $17. Loan platform slowdown. Moderate profile exclusion maintained.
PYPL HARD-EXCLUDED: Bearish signal, analyst hold, insider distributing. Layoffs news overhang. Exclusion confirmed.
RIVN HARD-EXCLUDED: No PEG (negative earnings), RSI=13.1 in distress zone, Tesla competition. Inappropriate for moderate mandate.
20% CASH BUFFER: Maintained. VIX=18.06 and Fear & Greed=42 confirm cautious positioning is warranted. Deploy on dips if macro clears.
